commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=27dbe39f1a1d318c68ce554c998b95d6dcdeaad1)
commands.review_summary:
The branch replaces Sharp with Photon for OpenClaw image operations, removes WhatsApp Jimp package surfaces, precomputes WhatsApp image preview fields, and updates shrinkwrap generation, docs, tests, and changelog.

PR surface: Source -446, Tests +180, Docs +3, Config +2, Other -1241. Total -1502 across 31 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes for review purposes: the compatibility blockers are source-reproducible from the PR diff against current main and the pinned Photon/Baileys dependency contracts, though I did not run tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the central blocker is the maintainer product/security decision for image format compatibility and upgrade behavior, not a purely mechanical repair.

target.title: Replace Sharp image backend with Photon
target.body:
Summary
- Replace the packaged Sharp image backend with Photon for metadata, resize-to-JPEG, PNG alpha-preserving resize, and EXIF orientation normalization.
- Keep HEIC/HEIF/AVIF conversion on system tools only, with docs updated for the converter requirement.
- Remove installed Sharp package surfaces and the WhatsApp Jimp fallback dependency; keep Baileys' Sharp/Jimp peers optional in generated shrinkwrap metadata.
- Prepopulate WhatsApp image thumbnails/dimensions via OpenClaw image ops before Baileys upload on both direct outbound sends and inbound/auto-reply `sendMedia` replies.
- Move test image generation off Sharp and add real fixture coverage for Photon edge cases, including grayscale-alpha PNG and PNG compression level handling.

Verification
- pnpm build
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/image-ops.input-guard.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.log.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts src/media/store.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/media.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply.web-auto-reply.compresses-common-formats-jpeg-cap.test.ts test/scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
  - 16 files passed, 335 tests passed
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.streams-inbound-messages.test-support.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts test/scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/package-manifest.contract.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose
  - 5 files passed, 149 tests passed
- env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed
- pnpm why sharp --prod --depth 6
  - no output
- pnpm why jimp --prod --depth 6
  - no output
- rg -n 'node_modules/jimp|node_modules/@jimp|@jimp|node_modules/sharp|node_modules/@img/sharp|@img/sharp' extensions/whatsapp/npm-shrinkwrap.json package.json npm-shrinkwrap.json pnpm-lock.yaml
  - no matches
- git diff --check
- autoreview --mode local
  - clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported

Real image comparison against Sharp
- tesco-shop.jpg source sharp=1200x630 photon=1200x630 jpeg sharp=13700/320x168 photon=15800/320x168
- mobile-ui-screenshot.png source sharp=360x727 photon=360x727 jpeg sharp=5746/158x320 photon=7206/158x320
- roborock-screenshot.jpg source sharp=435x685 photon=435x685 jpeg sharp=12914/203x320 photon=16074/203x320
- whatsapp-openclaw.jpg source sharp=471x1024 photon=471x1024 jpeg sharp=11612/147x320 photon=13419/147x320
- openclaw-logo-text.png source sharp=1768x363 photon=1768x363 jpeg sharp=6940/320x66 photon=9512/320x66 png sharp=19058/320x66 photon=22127/320x66

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OpenClaw image processing no longer installs or imports Sharp for core/WhatsApp image paths, and the WhatsApp package no longer installs Jimp. Photon handles core resize/metadata/orientation/PNG work, external tools handle HEIC/HEIF/AVIF conversion, and WhatsApp image uploads keep thumbnails/dimensions without Baileys' Sharp/Jimp fallback.
Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout, Node/pnpm repo build, Photon backend, temporary isolated Sharp install only for fixture comparison.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm build; focused Vitest commands above; env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed; Sharp/Jimp dependency/package scans; real-image comparison script; autoreview local.
Evidence after fix: build passed; focused tests passed 16/16 files and 335/335 tests; follow-up WhatsApp/contract tests passed 5/5 files and 149/149 tests; changed gate passed; Sharp and Jimp prod dependency trees empty; installed package scans had no Sharp/Jimp matches; real-image dimensions matched Sharp across five repository fixtures; autoreview clean.
Observed result after fix: Photon output preserved source and resized dimensions across JPEG/PNG/WebP fixture paths; WhatsApp direct and inbound reply image payloads now include precomputed jpegThumbnail, width, and height before Baileys upload; generated shrinkwraps omit installed Sharp/Jimp packages while preserving Baileys peer optionality.
What was not tested: Live WhatsApp send against a linked account; cross-platform HEIC/HEIF converter availability beyond the macOS sips conversion test path; pixel-level perceptual equivalence against Sharp beyond dimension and encoded-size comparison.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
